text.title: Ruby ActiveRecord Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real Rails apps.

antipattern.symptom: Index endpoint with 50 orders fires 51 queries; p99 latency spikes.
antipattern.fix: Add `.includes(:customer, :items)` at the query origin; bullet gem in dev to catch new occurrences.

antipattern.symptom: Order created but inventory not decremented; manual reconciliation; customer complaint.
antipattern.root-cause: Two AR writes outside transaction; second failed mid-call.
antipattern.fix: Wrap in `ActiveRecord::Base.transaction { order = Order.create!(...); InventoryItem.find(...).update!(qty: q - 1) }`.

antipattern.symptom: Query `Order.where(status: 'pending')` returns empty after migration; new typo `pendnig` silent.
antipattern.root-cause: No enum; status is a free string column.
antipattern.fix: Add `enum status: { pending: 0, paid: 1, shipped: 2, cancelled: 3 }`; queries become `Order.pending`.

antipattern.symptom: Reports show events at wrong hour; cron job fires 2h early after server moved to UTC.
antipattern.root-cause: `Time.now` used; Rails app config is `config.time_zone = 'Europe/Kyiv'` but Time.now ignores it.
antipattern.fix: Replace all Time.now → Time.current; Date.today → Date.current; in cops/rubocop enable Rails/TimeZone.
